lotYield.description: Per-lot wafer-sort yield and SPC alert state so a downstream SPC memo, CR, or gate review can cite the result structurally.
lotYield.schema: { lot: string, wafers: int, dieTested: int, diePass: int, lotMeanYield: number, spcAlert: string, disposition: string }

criteria.criterion: PASS if lot-mean wafer-sort yield is computed as passing die divided by total testable die on a per-wafer basis and averaged to the lot. FAIL if the yield basis (e.g. gross vs net die, tested vs total) is wrong or inconsistent.

PASS if the lot-mean yield is compared against the 88% SPC baseline (soft) and the 85% contractual foundry floor, with the correct consequence (investigation vs credit). FAIL if the baselines are confused or misapplied.

PASS if the SPC alert is evaluated as −2σ below the 12-lot rolling mean and the four Western Electric Rules are applied to the control chart. FAIL if the alert logic is wrong or the rolling window is misdefined.
PASS if SPC alerts are attributed to the Sierra SPC Analyzer as the sole authorized alert-generation mechanism. FAIL if alerts are computed by an ad-hoc/unnamed method.
PASS if wafer counts, per-wafer die counts, and lot totals reconcile arithmetically across the report. FAIL if the die accounting does not add up.

PASS if an excursion is accompanied by spatial/bin analysis (edge vs centre, bin Pareto, wafer-map correlation) that localizes the loss. FAIL if an excursion is reported with no failure-mode breakdown.
PASS if the report keeps wafer-sort (WS) yield distinct from OSAT final-test (FT) yield and only pulls FT in as a joint-RCA input, per SOP scope. FAIL if WS and FT yields are conflated.
PASS if a flagged lot is routed to the defined triage/quality-hold owner per Section 5 rather than dispositioned informally. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if a sustained or assignable-cause excursion references the joint foundry root-cause / change-request process. FAIL if a systematic excursion is closed with no corrective path.
PASS if the lot disposition (release / hold / rework / CR) follows from the yield and SPC state. FAIL if the disposition contradicts the data.
PASS if the report cites the Foundry Yield SPC SOP (SUM-YLD-SOP-004) as the source of the baseline and alert rules. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if spatial claims cite the underlying wafer-map data (e.g. SUM-SIE-WMAP-001-FAB). FAIL if edge/centre claims are made with no wafer-map source.
PASS if control-chart and trend claims cite the SPC data sets / analyzer (e.g. SUM-SIE-SPC-001, SUM-SIE-SPC-003). FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the 88% baseline and alert thresholds are attributed to the Stonefield Threshold Registry (SUM-REG-THR-001). FAIL otherwise.

PASS if linked SPC trigger memos, change requests, or conflict analyses (e.g. SUM-SIE-SPC-MEMO-2026-01, SUM-SIE-CR-001) and the affected risk (R-001) are cited where relevant. FAIL if referenced actions are unlinked.

PASS if every regulatory clause, standard, SOP, procedure, and controlled-document identifier cited in the output appears in the retrieved sources. FAIL if any citation is invented or cannot be traced.
PASS if every measurement, margin, count, score, and figure in the output traces to the retrieved data, tables, or tool results. FAIL if any number is unsupported.
PASS if the output contains every section the governing procedure requires for a foundry yield report. FAIL if a required section is missing or empty.
PASS if every factual claim in the output is supported by the retrieved sources and the exact data provided. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the output is written clearly and unambiguously, appropriate for a controlled quality record, with correct document and revision identifiers. FAIL otherwise.

trajectory.description: Provenance checks against the recorded retrieval trajectory for a foundry yield report.

required.reason: The yield baseline and SPC alert rules come from SUM-YLD-SOP-004; a yield report without it has no governed threshold.
The 88% baseline and alert values are registered in SUM-REG-THR-001 and must be traced there structurally.

forbidden.reason: A current lot determination must not be grounded in an archived legacy yield report as if it were the live baseline.
